senses.definition: the two major arteries in the neck that supply blood to the head and brain
examples: The doctor felt for the carotids to check the patient’s pulse.
A blockage in the carotids can increase the risk of stroke.

senses.definition: an older alternative name for the carotene pigment that gives carrots their orange color
examples: Carotin is another term sometimes used for certain carotenoids.
Carotin is related to the word carotene.

senses.definition: a yellowing of the skin caused by high levels of carotene in the blood
examples: Carotenaemia can occur after consuming large amounts of carrot juice.
The doctor diagnosed carotenaemia and advised dietary changes.

senses.definition: the condition of having an excess of carotene in the blood that causes yellowing of the skin
examples: Carotenemia and carotinemia are different spellings of the same condition.
A diet rich in orange vegetables can lead to carotinemia.

senses.definition: any member of a family of yellow to orange pigments produced by plants that are involved in photosynthesis
examples: Carotinoids include beta-carotene and lycopene.
Carotinoids are carotenoids with health benefits.

senses.definition: a group of plant pigments that are yellow to red and are important for photosynthesis and human nutrition
examples: Carotinoids help plants capture light for photosynthesis.
Dietary carotinoids include beta-carotene and lycopene.

senses.definition: an older or less common term referring to certain carotenoids or related compounds; often appears as a plural form in older texts
examples: Carotins were mentioned in early pigment studies.
The term carotins is rarely used in modern texts.

senses.definition: a specific hydrocarbon component within the carotenoid family, contributing to color in plants
examples: Carotol is a minor carotenoid variant found in some vegetables.
Research mentions carotol as part of the carotenoid spectrum.

senses.definition: the French word for carrot
examples: She bought a bunch of carottes at the market.
In French, carotte is the vegetable we call carrot in English.

senses.definition: a type of spiny pod used to make carob, derived from the carob tree
examples: Carouba is harvested to produce carob powder.
Carouba pods are used as a chocolate substitute.

senses.definition: the carob tree, Ceratonia siliqua, which produces carob pods
examples: The caroubier thrives in Mediterranean climates.
Caroubier wood is sometimes used ornamentally.

senses.definition: a noisy gathering or party with dancing and amusement; a carousel or rotating display in older usage
examples: The party turned into a lively carousal.
A merry carousal of music and dancing filled the hall.

senses.definition: plural of carousal; lively, boisterous celebrations
examples: The ship's crew spoke of wild carousals after the voyage.
Historical accounts describe elaborate carousals at courts.

senses.definition: to drink alcohol and celebrate in a noisy, lively way
examples: They caroused all night at the tavern.
People would carouse until dawn at the festival.

senses.definition: the act of drinking and celebrating boisterously in the past
examples: They caroused until 2 a.m. after the victory.
Friends caroused at the party last night.

senses.definition: a rotating circular platform with animals or seats for riding, often found at amusement parks; also a rotating display in a shop
examples: The carousel spins slowly with painted horses.
A carousel of products lined the shop window.

senses.definition: plural of carousel; amusement rides or rotating displays
examples: The fair had several carousels for children.
Different carousels showcased toys and sweets.

senses.definition: a person who goes on a drinking spree or wild party
examples: He was known as a frequent carouser at the tavern.
The town's carousers crowded the streets after the game.

senses.definition: people who go on drunken revels; partygoers
examples: The carousers filled the bar after the match.
A group of carousers wandered along the street.

senses.definition: to drink alcohol and celebrate boisterously
examples: He carouses with friends on weekends.
They carouse late into the night after parties.

senses.definition: the act of drinking and celebrating boisterously
examples: Carousing crowds spilled into the street.
They spent the night carousing at the festival.

senses.definition: in a way that is boisterous or festive, often loudly and socially
examples: They celebrated carousingly throughout the night.
The party room was decorated carousingly.

senses.definition: plural form referring to instances of carousing; boisterous celebrations
examples: Their carousings lasted until dawn.
histories describe many carousings at royal courts.

senses.definition: a freshwater fish; also a complaint or petty nagging
examples: We caught a carp while fishing.
Stop your carp about the delay.

senses.definition: to complain or find fault repeatedly
examples: Don't carp about every small issue.
They carp to the manager about the wait time.

senses.definition: a dish of very thin slices of raw meat or fish, usually served as an appetizer
examples: The carpaccio was topped with arugula and parmesan.
Vegetarian carpaccio uses beetroot or mushrooms.
